2606.00674.title: The Paradox of Outcome Optimization: A Causal Information-Theoretic Bound on Reasoning Shortcuts in LLMs
2606.00674.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) aligned via outcome-based Reinforcement Learning (RL) frequently exhibit a critical failure mode: they achieve high performance on in-distribution benchmarks while demonstrating brittle reasoning capabilities on out-of-distribution (OOD) tasks. We term this phenomenon Reward-Induced Manifold Collapse. We establish a theoretical framework bridging Structural Causal Models (SCM) and the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle to explain this paradox. We define reasoning as a high-complexity causal process and shortcut learning as the exploitation of low-complexity spurious correlations. Under the implicit inductive bias of Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD), models optimized for outcome rewards are biased toward shortcut solutions whenever the training distribution allows for a ``Markovian Screening'' of the true causal mechanism. We derive a new generalization bound based on Semantic Coverage Measure ($η$) rather than sample size, showing why data scaling on homogeneous distributions may fail to correct reasoning flaws. We also show that Process Reward Models (PRMs) function as Topological Filters, enforcing step-wise mutual information constraints that render the low-complexity shortcut manifold inadmissible. These results provide a mathematical grounding for the role of process supervision beyond simple credit assignment.
2606.00674.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）透過基於結果的強化學習（RL）進行對齊時，經常顯示出一種關鍵的失效模式：它們在內部分佈基準上表現出高效能，但在外部分佈（OOD）任務上卻展現出脆弱的推理能力。我們將這一現象稱為獎勵誘導的流形崩潰。我們建立了一個理論框架，將結構因果模型（SCM）與信息瓶頸（IB）原則聯繫起來，以解釋這一悖論。我們將推理定義為一個高複雜度的因果過程，而捷徑學習則被定義為利用低複雜度的虛假相關性。在隨機梯度下降（SGD）的隱性歸納偏見下，為結果獎勵而優化的模型在訓練分佈允許對真實因果機制進行“馬爾可夫篩選”的情況下，會偏向捷徑解決方案。我們基於語義覆蓋度量（$η$）而非樣本大小推導出一個新的泛化界限，顯示為何在同質分佈上的數據擴展可能無法修正推理缺陷。我們還展示了過程獎勵模型（PRMs）作為拓撲過濾器的功能，強制執行逐步的互信息約束，使低複雜度的捷徑流形不可接受。這些結果為過程監督在簡單的信用分配之外的角色提供了數學基礎。
2606.00674.author: Zihan Chen et.al.
2606.00674.authors: Zihan Chen, Yiming Zhang, Wenxiang Geng, Zenghui Ding, Yining Sun
